scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B — Both PR #312 (Alan) and PR #313 (Sarah) alter the events table (Alan adds webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah adds delivery_state and processor_version).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. She’s a part‑time contractor who works Monday/Wednesday/Friday and is not scheduled (and is marked off) on Thursday March 13.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — Josh told Acme we'd deliver SAML SSO after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out and the shipped work only includes JWTs and OAuth2 (Google/GitHub), so SAML isn’t covered.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed four steps (including "first milestone," the prior activation trigger), so "onboarding completion" now measures a different, shorter journey than the old 8‑step flow.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace says staging-3 was created for load testing (last deploy Jan 22) but was never actually used for load testing.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — Each ticket lists "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25" in the workspace, so all three customers were in migration batch-7 on Feb 25.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — Two. The design doc explicitly lists two open questions: (1) cursor vs offset pagination and (2) webhook payload format (nested vs flat).

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace notes the migration script is the "rosetta stone file" at src/billing/mapping.rs, which maps plan/tier/subscription.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Customer research shows all three prospects said CSV is sufficient; no customer requested PDF, and the Excel ask came from sales, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test stats show 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%), with 280 unit (82%) and 55 integration (16%) tests.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth so they get JWT benefits (rotation/revocation) while keeping the API-key-like simplicity for developers ("You can have JWT tokens that feel like API keys with long-lived tokens and bearer auth").

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text:
C

The plan notes there's no delivery confirmation mechanism — a customer's endpoint can return 200 without actually processing the event, so deliveries can appear successful while silently failing and only surface later (e.g., during invoice reconciliation).

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi. The workspace notes “no one has asked the customer perspective yet” and that Takeshi at NovaTech offered to review the proposal and responds within 2 hours, so loop him in.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — ~2.75 hours/week. The workspace sums: ~1h (hardcoded URLs) + 45m (retry logic) + ~1h (generic error messages); the skipped test causes missed bugs (no extra weekly time quantified).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc). Josh's sales notes list both asks, and Sarah already has a webhook signature module so adding Ed25519 is localized and cheap (≈ half a day).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even at feature 5. Solve 5N = 15 + 2N → 3N = 15 → N = 5, so costs equal at 5 features; net benefit begins at feature 6.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace shows this is a spec ambiguity: docs says "Display subscription start date" which can mean signup date (Jan 15, what Acme expects) or billing-period start (Feb 1, what engineering implemented). Investigation concluded it's a spec/UI issue and can be fixed with a 1‑line label change or showing both dates.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Because adding annual billing touches core billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning) and would interfere with the Billing Migration work that has a hard March 31 target and no buffer. Memory shows the migration is already blocked on reconciliation (risk of missing Mar 31), so this scope increase would directly conflict with that launch.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring detected the issue in 12 minutes, and the root cause is a process gap (the migration wasn't tested against production-scale data with full historical records, which missed legacy accounts lacking plan_change_date).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec, which is 95% of the 100 req/sec Stripe limit at baseline; with typical 1.5–2x spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the 100 limit and will produce 429 errors.
